title: An Alligator Parked Itself at a South Carolina Front Door
script: Alligator at the door! In Moncks Corner, South Carolina, a homeowner stepped out to find their front porch occupied — by a very large alligator, apparently going nowhere. Not moving. Not interested in leaving. Just posted up. The Berkeley County Sheriff's Office sent an animal control officer. The officer took one look and immediately called for backup: the South Carolina Department of Natural Resources. Because that's the threshold where 'we need SCDNR' — when even the animal control professionals say it's too big. Together, they secured the alligator and relocated it to a more appropriate setting. The homeowner was not injured. The alligator was not harmed. The front door is now unblocked. But this is South Carolina in June — why are we even surprised anymore?
